mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeflow
mission.description: Kubeflow is a comprehensive machine learning toolkit designed for Kubernetes, enabling users to build, deploy, and manage machine learning workflows. It is particularly useful for AI platform teams looking for a modular and scalable solution. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for Kubernetes expertise.

steps.title: Install the Kubeflow CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kubeflow CLI, which is essential for managing your Kubeflow deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/releases/download/v1.10.0/kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv kubeflow /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Kubeflow CLI for Linux.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster in your preferred cloud provider with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kubeflow-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch credentials for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster to verify their status.

steps.title: Install Kubeflow
steps.description:
This step deploys Kubeflow to your Kubernetes cluster using the CLI.

```bash

kubeflow install --version v1.10.0

```

> Install Kubeflow version 1.10.0.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Kubeflow deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/monitoring/monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/autoscaler/autoscaler.yaml

```

> Deploy monitoring components.
> Deploy autoscaling components.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kubeflow
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kubeflow from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

kubeflow uninstall

gcloud container clusters delete kubeflow-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstall Kubeflow from the cluster.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubeflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step includes draining nodes if necessary, performing the upgrade, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get pods --all-namespaces

kubeflow upgrade --version v1.10.0

kubectl get pods --all-namespaces

```

> Check the status of all pods before upgrading.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeflow install' command fails with 'insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the installation.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or size of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes' returns 'NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** One or more nodes are not in a ready state due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the node status and logs, and ensure that the nodes have sufficient resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeflow uninstall' command fails with 'resource not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubeflow installation may not have been completed successfully.

**Fix:**
Verify the installation status and ensure all components are correctly deployed.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeflow upgrade' command fails with 'version not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified version may not be available in the repository.

**Fix:**
Check the available versions and ensure you are using a valid version.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new ML frameworks and improved integration with existing tools.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain APIs related to model serving.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.9, ensure to migrate any custom configurations to the new API specifications.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of running pods and services when executing 'kubectl get pods --all-namespaces'.

prerequisites.resources: Minimum 3 nodes with 2 vCPU and 4GB RAM each
prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
